node.title: Uber | Data Engineer | Phone Screen | CodeSignal | Machine Coding | Queue design

topic.title: Uber | Data Engineer | Phone Screen | CodeSignal | Machine Coding | Queue design

post.content:
This was for the data engineer 2 position at Uber,Bangalore.
It was a 90 min Zoom call, with a single machine coding question:

Design an In-Memory Pull Based Queue Library:

Use Cases to be supported:

1.Multiple queues maintained by the Library
2.Each queue must support multiple publishers and subscribers. 
3.Each queue has a maximum retention period beyond which a message in the queue should not reside in memory.
4.Each message inside the queue can have an optional TTL value. Any message with expired TTL should not be consumed by any subscriber and should not reside in the memory as well.
5.Each consumer should read all the messages.

First 30 minutes were devoted to discussing the implementation approach, next 60 minutes to coding the solution.

Looking for ideas for a good solution.
I have a Data Engineer interview, but nowhere have they mentioned about LLDs for Data Engineering. \nDo I need to prepare for this?

1. A doubly linked list
2. A priority queue with key = expiration time and reference to the list node. A separate thread need to check the next TTL to be expired and can sleep or can check every second if there are expired keys. If expired keys refers to any consumer iterator, then that has to move. Put operation can also be used to do this task. However, that can increase the latency.
3. Each consumer will require its own iterator which will point to the list node. It should start either with head or tail based on requirement.
4. If a key has been read by all the consumers, if that also has to go away, a index will be required for each message and before minIndex, values can be removed from memory.
